Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for order to apportion and distribute funds.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:56:33 a.m. and concluding at 10:08:40 a.m.

Plaintiffs Robert Wilhite and Patsy Wilhite represented by Kimberly Chu, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Owens-Corning Fiberglass Coporation not appearing.

The matter is submitted.
The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, Plaintiff's Motion for Order to Apportion and Distribute is GRANTED.

The following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds (both litigation funds and funds from claims against bankrupt asbestos defendant trusts) negotiated after the death of ROBERT WUKGHITE in the following proportions:

Estate of ROBERT WILHITE, deceased
Asbestos-Injured Decedent: 25%

Estate of DARLENE MILLS, deceased
Wrongful Death Claimant: 50%

JENNIFER REYES
Wrongful Death Claimant: 25%

TOTAL: 100%

The signature of GAIL DUGGER shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and the wrongful death claims with defedantns in the filed asbestos personal-injury-survival and wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts established for asbestos victims.

Proposed order signed as modified.

Prove-Up Hearing As To Deft Western Macarthur By Plft Robert Whilwhite (Asbestos)
Off Calendar Mar-28-2002 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: CHRIS ANDREAS, Esq. of Brayton-Purcell, 415/898-1555 on behalf of plaintiff(s). No appearance on behalf of Western MacArthur. Request for judgment in favor of the plaintiff ROBERT W. WILHITE and against defendant WESTERN MacARTHUR CO. in the sum of $179,200.00 and judgment in favor of plaintiff PATSY WILHITE and against defendant WESTERN MacARTHUR CO. in the sum of $150,000.00 plus costs and interest as provided by law, in an amount to be determined later by the Court was made. The Court took matter Under Submission as of Mar-28-2002 by ALFRED G. CHIANTELLI. JUDGE CHIANTELLI; CLERK: V. MU; REPORTER: M. TORREANO, #8600 (503)
